content.text: Answer FOUR questions in detail. State carefully any results used without proof.

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Let \(g_n \to g\) and \(h_n \to h\) uniformly on \(X\). Prove that if \(g\) and \(h\) are bounded, then \(g_nh_n \to gh\) uniformly on \(X\).

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Let \(f:[a,b]\to\mathbb{R}\) be continuous. By considering the square-root or otherwise, prove that if \(f\) is non-negative then there exists a sequence of non-negative polynomials converging uniformly to \(f\) on \([a,b]\).

problem.text: Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be an equicontinuous family of real-valued functions on the connected space \(X\). By considering the set of points at which the family \(\mathcal{F}\) is bounded, prove that if \(\mathcal{F}\) is bounded at one point of \(X\) then it is bounded at all points of \(X\).
\((f_n:n\geq 0)\) is a sequence of measurable real-valued functions. Prove that each of the following sets is measurable:

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: the set \(A\) comprising all points \(\omega\) for which the sequence of values \(f_n(\omega)\) is eventually positive;

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: the set \(B\) comprising all points \(\omega\) for which the sequence of values \(f_n(\omega)\) changes sign infinitely often.

problem.text: \((\Omega,\mathcal{F},\mu)\) is a measure space on which \((f_n:n\geq 0)\) is a sequence of Lebesgue-measurable functions converging uniformly to \(f\). Prove that if \(\mu(\Omega)<\infty\) then it follows that \(f\) is integrable and \(\int_\Omega f_n\,d\mu\to\int_\Omega f\,d\mu\). Show by example that if \(\mu(\Omega)=\infty\) then the same conclusion may not follow.

Let \((f_n:n\geq 0)\) be a uniformly-bounded sequence of Riemann-integrable functions on \([a,b]\). If \(f_n\to 0\) pointwise, does it follow that \(\int_a^b f_n\to 0\)? Proof or counterexample required.
